title: Multi-LLM-based augmentation and synthetic data generation of construction schedules and task descriptions with SLM-as-a-judge assessment
task: 进度数据增强与合成数据生成
contribution: 多LLM框架生成高质量施工进度合成数据，Claude-3.5-Sonnet在进度增强任务中质量达77%
abstract: This study proposes an integrated multi-LLM framework for augmenting and generating construction schedule data and task descriptions, combining Role-Guided Modular Prompt Chaining (RGPC) for schedule enhancement, a multi-LLM pipeline for synthetic data generation, and an SLM-as-a-Judge rubric-based evaluation module using locally deployed small language models for privacy-preserving assessment. Experimental results demonstrate that Claude-3.5-Sonnet achieved 77% quality in augmented schedule generation, Gemini-2.0-Flash reached 92% in synthetic schedule generation, and DeepSeek-R1 provided the best balance of quality and diversity in synthetic construction task description generation.
methodology: The framework integrates Role-Guided Modular Prompt Chaining (RGPC) for schedule augmentation, a multi-LLM pipeline with zero-shot and role-based prompting for synthetic schedule and task description generation, and an SLM-as-a-Judge module using locally deployed models (Flow Judge, ArmoRM, Prometheus 2) for rubric-based quality evaluation across structural, semantic, and logical dimensions.
results: Claude-3.5-Sonnet achieved the highest overall quality score of 77% for schedule augmentation, Gemini-2.0-Flash led synthetic schedule generation at 92%, and DeepSeek-R1 achieved the best quality-diversity trade-off (EDA score of 8) for construction task description generation with outputs exceeding 92% quality while maintaining low Self-BLEU diversity scores below 6.5%.
